response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong web, systems, and craftsmanship credentials, but the role requires deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His C++ exposure is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, with the past decade spent primarily in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at scale, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial protocol experience, all of which are core to this position.
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering experience (15+ years) spanning web, embedded, and desktop systems
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec) using async I/O and fibers at Dunnhumby
Strong testing and mentoring culture: TDD, open-source test tooling, workshops, and training courses
concerns: Minimal recent C++ experience; last professional C++ role ended in 2015 and C++ is absent from the skills list
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or IEC 62443 secure-development background
Career trajectory centers on web/full-stack product work, misaligned with an on-prem industrial C++ platform role

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity/SDL or IoT protocol experience
Strong general systems and low-latency engineering background
